decks.notes: This deck focuses on controlling the game by using disruptive spells like Thoughtseize, Duress, and Fatal Push to disrupt the opponent's strategy. It also utilizes powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Professor Dellian Fel to dominate the board. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent by using graveyard recursion and generating card advantage through abilities like Unholy Annex / Ritual Chamber. Boseiju, Who Endures and Tear Asunder provide additional utility options, while Mutavault and Badgermole Cub offer versatility in combat situations. Overall, the deck aims to grind out victories through attrition and resource management.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to utilize cheap spells and efficient creatures to apply pressure on the opponent quickly. With a mix of burn spells, card draw, and evasive threats like Stormchaser's Talent and Vivi Ornitier, the deck aims to control the tempo of the game and overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize. The inclusion of counter magic like Spell Pierce and Divide by Zero provides versatility and disruption to protect the aggressive game plan. This deck focuses on aggression and tempo to secure the victory.

This Rakdos Aggro deck focuses on quick and aggressive plays, utilizing cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to disrupt the opponent while deploying threats like Graveyard Trespasser and Bloodtithe Harvester. Fable of the Mirror-Breaker and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse provide additional value and pressure. The deck also includes burn spells like Torch the Tower to deal direct damage to the opponent. Mutavault and Fear of Missing Out help to maintain pressure on the opponent while controlling the board. Overall, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast and efficient creatures and spells.

This deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a combination of efficient creatures and disruptive elements. The focus is on deploying threats quickly and efficiently, while also hindering the opponent's game plan with cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Archon of Emeria. The inclusion of Collected Company allows for explosive plays and the ability to hit key creatures at instant speed. With a mix of powerful creatures and disruptive spells, this Selesnya Aggro deck looks to apply pressure early and consistently until victory is achieved.

The main concept of this Red Deck Wins archetype is to overwhelm the opponent by playing aggressive creatures and burn spells to deal damage quickly. Cards like Emberheart Challenger and Monastery Swiftspear help to push the tempo in the early game, while cards like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage provide removal and direct damage to close out the game. The deck aims to be fast and efficient, taking advantage of the opponent's weaknesses and punishing any slow starts. This deck thrives on putting pressure on the opponent and staying ahead in the race to reduce their life total to zero.

This UW Control deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of counterspells, removal, and card advantage. Using cards like Narset, Parter of Veils and Teferi, Hero of Dominaria to disrupt opponents' strategies, while also utilizing board wipes like Supreme Verdict to clear the battlefield. The deck also includes card draw spells like Consult the Star Charts and Meticulous Archive to maintain a steady stream of resources. Additionally, it has land destruction options like Field of Ruin to disrupt opponents' mana bases. Overall, the main goal is to outlast opponents and win through incremental advantage.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's board and hand through a variety of cards that disrupt their strategy and provide card advantage. It aims to maintain a solid defense by utilizing counterspells like Dovin's Veto and Supreme Verdict, while also generating value through cards like Consult the Star Charts and Teferi, Hero of Dominaria. The deck also has a win condition with cards like Elspeth, Storm Slayer and The Wandering Emperor, allowing for a versatile and adaptive playstyle that can outmaneuver opponents.

The main idea of this deck is to control the game by using a combination of removal spells, counterspells, and card draw. The deck focuses on maintaining card advantage and disrupting the opponent's strategy while gradually building up resources. It aims to control the board with cards like Supreme Verdict and Teferi, Hero of Dominaria, while also drawing into answers with Consult the Star Charts and Narset, Parter of Veils. The deck is designed to outlast the opponent and win through attrition by efficiently managing resources and countering threats.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on quickly and efficiently dealing damage to the opponent, utilizing low-cost spells and creatures to maintain pressure. By playing aggressive creatures like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear, alongside cheap spells like Burst Lightning and Sleight of Hand, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent early in the game. The inclusion of cards like Academic Dispute and Stormchaser's Talent provide additional versatility and card advantage, allowing the deck to adapt to various board states and maintain momentum throughout the match.

This deck focuses on building a fast and aggressive strategy to overwhelm the opponent with early game creatures and burn spells. The key idea is to play efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage alongside burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage to quickly reduce the opponent's life total. The inclusion of powerful land cards like Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins helps provide additional reach and damage. By applying constant pressure and utilizing direct damage, this deck aims to win the game before the opponent can stabilize.

This UW Control deck focuses on using powerful control spells and card draw to outlast the opponent and disrupt their game plan. The deck includes powerful finishers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and The Wandering Emperor, along with a variety of counterspells and removal spells to control the board. The deck also utilizes card draw spells like Memory Deluge and Consult the Star Charts to maintain card advantage and find answers to threats. With a focus on controlling the game and staying ahead on resources, this deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent.

The deck focuses on utilizing aggressive spells and creatures, such as Stormchaser's Talent and Combustion Technique, to quickly deal damage to the opponent. It also has cards like Divide by Zero and Consider for card advantage and control options. The mana base is built around UR dual lands and efficient spells like Gran-Gran and Vivi Ornitier to maintain tempo and pressure on the opponent. The key strategy is to apply constant pressure, control the board, and finish off the opponent with direct damage spells or powerful creatures.

The deck focuses on utilizing Lotus Field to generate significant amounts of mana quickly, allowing for explosive plays and the potential to combo off and win the game in a single turn. It includes cards like Thespian's Stage, Hidden Strings, and Vizier of Tumbling Sands to untap Lotus Field and generate even more mana. The deck also includes card draw and selection spells like Sleight of Hand and Pore Over the Pages to find the combo pieces quickly. Overall, the deck aims to assemble its combo pieces and win the game before the opponent has a chance to react.

The deck focuses on casting cheap spells to quickly fill the graveyard with Arclight Phoenixes, which can be brought back to the battlefield for massive damage. The deck includes cards like Opt, Treasure Cruise, and Fiery Impulse to help cycle through the deck quickly and bring back the Phoenixes. With the help of cheap spells and card draw, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a flurry of flying Phoenixes and burn spells. It also includes some control elements like Lightning Axe and Torch the Tower to deal with opposing threats.

This deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with spells to bring back Arclight Phoenix from the grave. It utilizes cheap card draw and burn spells to both dig for the Phoenix and disrupt the opponent's game plan. By using efficient spells and graveyard recursion, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a steady stream of threats that can quickly close out the game. It also includes cards like Treasure Cruise and Opt to ensure a consistent flow of spells to fuel the Phoenix's return.

This deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo by controlling the board with removal spells like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize while ramping up with Monument to Endurance. The main win condition is to create a powerful board state with Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II, supported by protection from Guardian of New Benalia and Iron-Shield Elf. The deck also includes utility lands like The Mycosynth Gardens and Geier Reach Sanitarium to further support the combo strategy. Overall, this deck aims to create a dominant board presence and overwhelm opponents with its combo potential.
